channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Clean Energy Team Wins Salt River Project Election in Arizona
item.description: Proponents of renewable power will control the Phoenix area utility’s policymaking for the first time after they won an unusually contentious race that drew attention from national groups.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:credit: Vanessa Abbitt/The Republic, via Imagn Images
item.media:description: The Salt River Project Agua Fria Generating Station in Glendale, Ariz., powers much of the Phoenix valley.

item.title: U.S. Fertility Rates Drop to Another Record Low
item.description: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007, in large part because of a plunge among teenagers.
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise
item.media:credit: Markus Scholz/picture alliance, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007.

item.title: The Fragile Cease-Fire in Iran
item.description: President Trump announced a conditional cease-fire with Israel on Iran, but the fundamental issues that led to the war remain unresolved.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Stephanie Swart, Ray Whitehouse, Paul Abowd and Jon Miller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: No Charges for Wisconsin Mayor Who Removed Ballot Drop Box in 2024
item.description: A special prosecutor said a review of the incident found there was not sufficient evidence to charge Doug Diny, the mayor of Wausau.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Doug Diny, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Mayor Doug Diny removing a ballot box in Wausau, Wis., in a 2024 photograph he provided.

item.title: Hawaii Doctor Convicted of Attempted Manslaughter in Attack on Wife
item.description: Gerhardt Konig, 47, an anesthesiologist from Maui, attacked his wife, Arielle Konig, on a hiking trail near Honolulu, prosecutors said. He claimed it was self-defense.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Mengshin Lin
item.media:description: Gerhardt Konig on Tuesday during closing arguments in his attempted-murder trial in Honolulu.

item.title: Martin Gugino, Activist Shoved by Buffalo Police at 2020 Protest, Dies
item.description: Mr. Gugino, 81, had filed a lawsuit in 2021 against the city of Buffalo and members of its police force after officers fractured his skull at a Black Lives Matter protest.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

item.title: Ohio Man Is First to Be Federally Convicted for Deepfake Porn
item.description: James Strahler II, 37, of Columbus, Ohio, had at least 10 victims, according to the authorities. He pleaded guilty to cyberstalking and other charges covered by the Take It Down Act.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk
item.media:credit: Sarah Silbiger for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Finds Himself With Fragile Iran Cease-Fire After the Scramble to Make a Deal
item.description: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Katie Rogers and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s efforts to circumvent the reality on the ground in the Middle East and move into a peace process have been hampered by an adversary that continues to hold leverage.

item.title: New Deadline Looms for U.S. and Iran as Truce Wavers
item.description: Fractures were already emerging in the limited cease-fire. Vice President JD Vance will lead a U.S. delegation in talks this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Office with President Trump last month. Mr. Vance is to lead a U.S. delegation to Islamabad for a meeting on Saturday with Iranian officials.

item.title: Who Is Funding the 2026 Midterm Elections? A Lot of the Money Is Untraceable.
item.description: A lot of the money flowing into the political system is ultimately untraceable.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The explosion in “gray money” is one of the defining trends in campaign finance.

item.title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
item.description: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: The construction site for the new White House ballroom last month.

item.title: Trump Administration Investigating L.A. Schools’ Gender Disclosure Policies
item.description: The investigation into the nation’s second-largest school district was prompted by a lawsuit from parents who say the policies contributed to their child’s death.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: In January, the Supreme Court heard arguments concerning transgender athletes. Groups involved in the parents’ rights movement have helped shape the Trump administration’s priorities, including the legal and institutional protections for transgender students.

item.title: Has Trump Met His War Goals in Iran?
item.description: On the first day of the pause, Iran fired missiles and launched drones in the region. It said an oil refinery on Lavan Island had been attacked. Israel continued its strikes in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump announced Wednesday he would move forward with diplomacy during a tenuous two-week cease-fire with Iran.

item.title: Tennessee Plant Is Fined $3.1 Million After Explosion That Killed 16
item.description: The fine, which comes six months after an explosion at the plant killed 16 people, is the largest ever issued by Tennessee’s occupational safety agency.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Austin Anthony for The New York Times
item.media:description: An ambulance at Accurate Energetic Systems last October.

item.title: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
item.description: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson and Steven Lee Myers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: California Supreme Court Orders Sheriff to Halt Election Investigation
item.description: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a candidate for governor, had seized ballots from a 2025 special election based on unsubstantiated claims of election irregularities.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Sarah Reingewirtz/Los Angeles Daily News, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Chad Bianco, the sheriff of Riverside County and a Republican candidate for California governor, seized election materials while pursuing an investigation into the 2025 election over the objections of the attorney general of California and the secretary of state.

item.title: Lawyer for Man Shot by ICE Says He Beat Murder Charge in El Salvador
item.description: The agency had been seeking him in Northern California, saying he was wanted for questioning in that country. On Wednesday, his lawyer said he was a victim of bad law enforcement work.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:credit: Andy Alfaro/The Modesto Bee, via ZUMA Press Wire
item.media:description: After the shooting in Patterson, Calif., law enforcement officers flooded the area.

item.title: As Trump Swings on Iran War, Republican Leaders in Congress Are Largely Silent
item.description: In a week in which President Trump has veered from threatening to wipe out Iranian civilization to declaring a cease-fire, Congress is out of session and lawmakers with the power to declare war are mostly in the dark.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Inside the Capitol last month, as lawmakers left Washington for a two-week break.

item.title: Military Families Bear the Burden of War, Again
item.description: Even as a two-week ceasefire takes hold, mothers in multigenerational military families — some veterans themselves — are anxious about what the war in Iran could mean for their children, as they face the uncertainty of another conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kassie Bracken, Luke Piotrowski and Caroline Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: For Trump, Resuming Combat in Iran Would Be Risky, Even if Cease-Fire Expires
item.description: President Trump knows that even if a cease-fire runs out with no final agreement on the issues dividing Washington and Tehran, the political risk of renewing hostilities is high.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday declared that the Iranians had been “humiliated and demoralized.”

item.title: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
item.description: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the post.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pam Bondi’s stumbles in handling the Epstein investigation played a pivotal role in President Trump’s decision to fire her.

item.title: What Trump Has Accomplished in Iran After 5 Weeks of War
item.description: President Trump said that “we have already met and exceeded” his military objectives. But his goals are largely unresolved.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump went to war with Iran pledging to prevent them from getting nuclear weapons.

item.title: Georgia, Wisconsin Elections Show Declining Appetite for Republican Candidates
item.description: A Republican won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s seat, but Democrats shifted the district 25 points to the left since the 2024 presidential race. Conservative candidates lost in Wisconsin, too.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Mike Stewart/Associated Press
item.media:description: Republican Clay Fuller speaks at a watch party after winning a special election for Georgia’s 14th Congressional District in Ringgold, Ga., on Tuesday.

item.title: Stung by Voters, Republican Legislators Move to Curb Citizen Initiatives
item.description: After citizens in Republican states used ballot measures to protect abortion, expand Medicaid and raise the minimum wage, statehouses are moving to make such initiatives much harder.
item.dc:creator: Kate Zernike
item.media:credit: Christopher Smith for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Missouri State Capitol in Jefferson City. Citizen groups in the state are collecting signatures to protect the initiative process.
